What We Monitor / Conveyors

Asset type

Conveyor monitoring

SAM4 monitors belt conveyors, screw conveyors, and chain conveyors from the motor control cabinet. detecting bearing degradation, misalignment, blockage, and overload conditions without sensors on the conveyor itself.

Talk to an engineer See What We Detect

Why monitor conveyors

When the conveyor stops, the entire process stops

Conveyors are the arteries of bulk material handling. They connect crushers to screens, mills to stockpiles, and production lines to dispatch. A single conveyor failure can halt an entire processing chain. Most conveyors have no condition monitoring beyond visual inspection.

€20–100K

typical cost of an unplanned conveyor failure, including production loss, emergency repair, and material spillage cleanup.

35%

of conveyor drive failures are bearing-related and detectable weeks in advance through electrical signature analysis.

4–8 hrs

average downtime for an unplanned conveyor belt or drive failure in a mining operation.

What SAM4 detects on conveyors

Five fault types from one sensor location

Fault type What's happening How SAM4 detects it Typical lead time Confidence
Blockage Material buildup, chute blockage, or belt tracking issues increasing drive load Load signature shift: sustained increase in torque demand above baseline at equivalent speed Hours–days Full
Misalignment Drive shaft or coupling misalignment between motor, gearbox, and drive roller 2× running speed component in the current spectrum, progressive increase as coupling or shaft alignment degrades 2–6 weeks Full
Overload Excessive material feed rate or belt/chain tension issues exceeding drive capacity Current draw exceeding baseline thresholds at equivalent speed. Sustained high current above motor nameplate rating Hours–days Full
Bearing degradation Drive roller, idler, or gearbox bearing wear and lubrication breakdown Characteristic bearing fault frequencies (BPFO, BPFI sidebands) in the motor current spectrum, modulated by load zone position 3–8 weeks 75.0%
Stator winding degradation Insulation breakdown in stator windings from thermal cycling, contamination, or voltage stress Current imbalance between phases and rising negative sequence current components 4–12 weeks Full
Broken rotor bars Cracked or broken bars in the rotor cage from thermal fatigue or high starting currents Characteristic sidebands at (1±2s)f around supply frequency, where s is motor slip 4–8 weeks Full
Supply quality issues Voltage imbalance, harmonic distortion, or VFD-related interference affecting motor performance Voltage and current harmonic spectrum analysis, power factor deviation, and supply-synchronous patterns Continuous Full

Detection capability depends on drive topology, sampling rate, load variation, and operating conditions. See ESA detection constraints.

Detection boundaries

No condition monitoring technology detects everything. These are the known limitations for this asset type.

Electrical unbalance: 33.3% detection on conveyors. Supply-side anomalies can mask the fault signature.
Coupling unbalance: often masked by other drivetrain dynamics in conveyor applications.

Based on 1,874 resolved incidents (Jan 2024 – Feb 2026). See full detection transparency report →

Conveyor detections

Real faults caught on conveyors

Bearing Metals & Mining

Gearbox bearing fault on belt conveyor

Progressive gearbox bearing wear detected on a primary ore conveyor. Replacement planned during scheduled shutdown, avoiding 6-hour unplanned stoppage.

Read detection story →
Misalignment Pulp & Paper

Drive misalignment on screw conveyor

Coupling misalignment between motor and gearbox flagged by 2× component trend. Corrected before secondary bearing damage.

Read detection story →
Blockage Metals & Mining

Chute blockage on transfer conveyor

Load signature shift identified progressive material buildup in transfer chute. Cleared before belt damage or spillage event.

Read detection story →
Overload Metals & Mining

Overload on bucket elevator

Sustained current increase flagged excessive feed rate. Feed adjusted before chain tensioner failure.

Read detection story →
Electrical Pulp & Paper

Motor winding fault on conveyor drive

Phase imbalance trend flagged developing stator fault. Motor replaced during maintenance window instead of mid-shift.

Read detection story →
Mechanical Metals & Mining

Belt tracking issue on overland conveyor

Asymmetric load pattern revealed belt mistrack. Tracking corrected before edge damage required belt replacement.

Read detection story →
See all detection stories →

Installation on conveyors

Under 60 minutes. No access to the conveyor structure required.

1

Open the motor control cabinet

SAM4 installs at the MCC, the same panel your electricians already access. No confined space entry, no scaffolding, no process shutdown.

2

Clip sensor onto motor supply cables

Current and voltage sensors clip directly onto the existing cabling. No wiring changes. No interruption to the asset's operation.

3

Connect and commission

The SAM4 gateway connects via cellular (4G/LTE). No dependency on your IT network. Monitoring starts immediately, with first diagnostic results within 48 hours.

Installation photo: SAM4 sensor clipped onto conveyor drive motor cables in an MCC
Full installation guide → Security & network architecture →

Where conveyors are monitored

Industries using SAM4 on conveyors

Metals & Mining

Ore conveyors, transfer conveyors, overland conveyors, bucket elevators. Remote and harsh environments.

Pulp & Paper

Chip conveyors, bark conveyors, process material handling across mill operations.

Related asset types

Other asset types SAM4 monitors

Transmissions

Gearboxes, couplings, belt drives, chain drives

Pumps

Submersible, booster, process, and transfer pumps

Fans & Blowers

Ventilation fans, cooling fans, process blowers

Compressors

Gas, air, and process compressors
See all asset types →
